result.content: FINAL_ANSWER: To reconstruct the sequence, perform a post-order traversal of the initial tree. For each node u in the initial tree, identify the contiguous segments of its initial children [ui, ui+1, ..., uj] that are mapped to a single child v in the final tree. For each such segment, perform (j - i) merge operations: first merge ui with ui+1 to create a new node, then merge that new node with ui+2, and continue this process until only one node (representing the target child v) remains. The operations must be recorded in the order they are encountered during the post-order traversal to ensure that all children of a node are merged before any operations involving the node itself are processed. JUSTIFICATION: A post-order traversal ensures that all necessary merge operations within the subtrees of a node are completed before the node's own children are merged, maintaining the structural integrity required by the tree transformation rules.
